stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released 25-Apr-2026: standalone net profit Rs 229.71 cr (+234% YoY from Rs 69 cr), highest-ever quarterly NII at Rs 1,671 cr (+7% YoY), total income Rs 4,789.21 cr (+7%). Advances +23% YoY to Rs 1,14,232 cr; deposits +25% YoY to Rs 1,39,018 cr (CD ratio 82.2%). Asset quality sharply improved: GNPA 1.45% (vs 2.60% YoY), NNPA 0.39%. Board recommended Rs 1/share (10%) dividend. Stock at Rs 313.25 on 27-Apr-2026 (-2.54% on the day). Big overhang/catalyst: RBI on 02-Apr-2026 cleared Emirates NBD's $3B (~Rs 26,850 cr) acquisition of up to 74% in RBL Bank; CCI cleared; SEBI approval pending. Deal originally announced 18-Oct-2025.
stocks.sector_context: Private bank NIMs declined 15 bps to 3.87% in Q2 FY26; H2 FY26 recovery expected as deposit repricing post 50 bps repo cut materializes. System credit growth ~13.5% YoY in early 2026, led by mid-sized lenders and SFBs. Deposit growth lags credit; CD ratio at ~83% keeps funding costs elevated. Sector themes for FY26: NIM stabilization, asset-quality discipline, disciplined growth.
stocks.peer_comparison: Vs IDFC First Bank Q4 FY26: IDFC First PAT only +4.9% to Rs 319 cr but PPOP collapsed -41.6% YoY; IDFC First NIM 5.93% (AUM basis) vs RBL's 3.62%. RBL's headline profit growth (+234%) leads the mid-cap private bank cohort in % terms but off a low base and supported partly by provisioning reversals. The 115 bps YoY GNPA improvement is best-in-class in the cohort.
red_flags: NIM compressed 22 bps in Q4 FY26 driven by lower yield on advances
Profit recovery partially supported by provisioning reversals rather than purely organic core earnings
Emirates NBD deal still requires SEBI approval - regulatory tail risk and event-driven volatility around clearance/rejection news
Stock fell ~2.5% on 27-Apr post strong results, indicating sell-the-news / valuation digestion
catalysts: Pending SEBI approval of Emirates NBD up-to-74% stake acquisition at Rs 26,850 cr - re-rating catalyst on closure
Final dividend Rs 1/share subject to AGM approval
H2 FY26 NIM recovery thesis playing out across private banks

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released 27-28 Apr 2026: consolidated net profit Rs 502 cr (+390% YoY) BUT inflated by Rs 1,590 cr exceptional gains from legacy asset sales; FY26 PAT Rs 1,506 cr (FY26 standalone Rs 603 cr). NII +41% YoY to Rs 1,362 cr. AUM crossed Rs 1 lakh cr (Rs 1.01 lakh cr) with retail at 85% of mix. Stock hit all-time high Rs 1,966 on 28-Apr (intraday +6.7%); Rs 1,840.70 on 27-Apr-2026. Final dividend Rs 11/share recommended; Suhail Nathani reappointed; registered office change effective 30-Apr-2026. Earlier in April: board approved Rs 15,000 cr NCD issuance and a subsidiary amalgamation; director Gautam Doshi resigned. Company filed clarification denying IIFL MFI deal reports.
stocks.sector_context: RBI's NBFC Scale-Based Regulation Amendment Directions effective 01-Apr-2026: new classification, refined infrastructure-loan risk weights (capital relief), revised concentration-risk norms, dynamic 2FA mandate for non-recurring digital payments, and a proposed simple Rs 1 lakh cr asset threshold for Upper Layer NBFCs. PIRAMALFIN's Rs 1.01 lakh cr AUM is right at that border. Bajaj Finance Q4 PAT +23% to Rs 4,840 cr; Shriram Finance Q4 PAT +41% to Rs 3,014 cr - peers showed clean organic growth without one-off boosts.
stocks.peer_comparison: P/E ~41x vs Bajaj Finance 31.5x and Shriram Finance 23.7x - the most expensive in the cohort. TTM ROE only 0.94%-2.82% vs Bajaj Finance 18.46% and Cholamandalam 19.72% - structurally subpar return profile. Headline +390% PAT growth optically dwarfs Bajaj (+23%) and Shriram (+41%), but ex-Rs 1,590 cr exceptional gain core run-rate is materially weaker than peers.
red_flags: Provisions for legacy assets jumped to Rs 1,787 cr from Rs 531 cr YoY - ongoing stress in the older wholesale book
Q4 profit beat almost entirely driven by Rs 1,590 cr exceptional gain from asset sales, not core operations
P/E ~41x materially elevated vs Bajaj Finance and Shriram Finance
Jefferies cautious on valuation; expects subdued return ratios over the medium term
Director Gautam Doshi resigned during the quarter - governance/board churn signal
Stock at all-time high after sharp run-up - elevated short-term sell-the-news risk
catalysts: Rs 11/share final dividend pending AGM approval
Rs 15,000 cr NCD issuance (board approved) - funding-cost optimization
Retail-mix shift continuing (85% of AUM) and risky-asset run-down theme
Possible Upper Layer NBFC classification under new RBI rules - more scrutiny but signals scale

stocks.recent_news: Stock at Rs 802.15 on 28-Apr-2026 (intraday Rs 717.10 - Rs 818); m-cap Rs 20,435.57 cr. Up ~9% on heavy volumes on 28-Apr ahead of results, suggesting positioning for a positive Q4 surprise. Q4 FY26 board meeting + analyst call scheduled 04-May-2026 (call 7:30 PM IST with MD R. Mukundan and CFO N. Tirumalai) to approve results and consider dividend. Most recent reported quarter Q3 FY26 (Dec-2025) was a net loss of Rs 93 cr - first quarterly loss in 9 years; Q3 revenue Rs 3,550 cr (-1.11% YoY). Cheshire Salt Holdings and New Cheshire Salt Works (UK) dissolved on 14-Apr-2026 as part of UK structure simplification.
stocks.sector_context: Soda ash (~2/3 of TATACHEM volumes) flat at 1,222 CNY/T on 27-Apr-2026, +0.83% MoM but -13.21% YoY. Asia (China + India) prices remain pressured by oversupply and a slowing Chinese economy; falling caustic-soda prices pulling silicate-market demand away from soda ash. TATACHEM has cut soda ash prices 5+ times since April 2023. Long-term global soda ash market projected at 4.5% CAGR to USD 29.78 bn by 2033 - structurally OK but cyclically weak.
stocks.peer_comparison: Domestic peers GHCL and DCW are more soda-ash-pure-play; DCM Shriram (P/E ~26.9x) trades at a more attractive valuation than TATACHEM. TATACHEM's diversification into specialty silica, food-grade ingredients and agro-inputs gives a wider product footprint than soda-ash-focused peers but has not insulated earnings from the commodity-price cycle. Chemicals sector broadly facing the same demand/oversupply headwinds heading into Q4 FY26 print.
red_flags: Q3 FY26 reported a Rs 93 cr loss - first quarterly loss in 9 years; momentum bar low for Q4
Soda ash spot price -13.21% YoY with persistent China/India oversupply
Sustained price cuts (5+ times since April 2023) reflect ongoing pricing-power erosion
~9% pre-results rally on heavy volumes - elevated event risk if Q4 print disappoints
catalysts: Q4 FY26 + FY26 results, dividend decision and management call on 04-May-2026 - high-conviction near-term binary event
Potential soda ash volume/margin recovery in India and Kenya (analyst expectations)
UK structure simplification (Cheshire Salt entities dissolved 14-Apr-2026) and ongoing cost optimization
Long-term soda ash demand growth 4.5% CAGR (lithium-ion, solar-glass linkages)
